items.prompt_md: Why does validating a BST require passing a `(low, high)` range down the recursion, instead of just comparing each node to its immediate children?
options: Because a node must be bounded by ALL of its ancestors, not just its parent — a local check can pass at every parent-child pair while still being globally invalid
Because recursion cannot access a node's children without an explicit range argument
Because BSTs are not guaranteed to be balanced
Because Python cannot compare tree node values without a numeric bound
items.answer: Because a node must be bounded by ALL of its ancestors, not just its parent — a local check can pass at every parent-child pair while still being globally invalid
items.explanation_md: A node deep in a subtree is constrained by every ancestor above it, not only its direct parent. The shrinking (low, high) range carries that accumulated constraint down the recursion; a purely local parent-child comparison cannot see it.

items.prompt_md: You need to find the kth smallest value stored in a binary search tree. Which approach fits?
options: Walk the BST in-order (left, node, right) and stop once you've visited k nodes — the walk already visits values in ascending order
BFS level by level and take the kth node dequeued
Build a hash map of every value seen so far and look up the kth key
Two pointers starting from the tree's minimum and maximum leaves, closing inward
items.answer: Walk the BST in-order (left, node, right) and stop once you've visited k nodes — the walk already visits values in ascending order
items.explanation_md: A BST's shape guarantees an in-order traversal visits values in ascending sorted order, so 'kth smallest' is answered by counting nodes during a plain in-order walk — no separate sort needed.

items.prompt_md: What is the time and space complexity of validating a BST with the range-propagation DFS, for an n-node tree of height h?
options: O(n) time, O(h) space for the recursion stack
O(n log n) time, O(n) space, because every node's range must be recomputed from the root
O(n) time, O(n) space always, regardless of the tree's shape
items.answer: O(n) time, O(h) space for the recursion stack
items.explanation_md: Each node is visited once and does O(1) work checking its inherited range, so O(n) time overall. The only extra memory is the call stack, bounded by the tree's height — the same O(h) bound as any DFS on a tree.
